workingDeparture.notes: Friday uses the morning for RI and CT, then positions near Stamford / White Plains / Tallman before the 13:00 work window. After work, activate Tallman, then continue to the booked Edison, NJ hotel.

riActivation.preferredTiming: Friday morning before work from Rhode Island
riActivation.fallbackTiming: Only if the morning activation fails; otherwise do not spend Friday evening on RI.
riActivation.notes: Current plan assumes RI is complete Friday morning before continuing to CT and the lower-Hudson work position.

parkStopPlanning.meaning: For broad planning, count two hours per non-overnight park stop to cover leaving the highway, setup, activation, teardown, and returning to the route.

roveCounting.multiParkRule: Multi-entity activations count once per unique POTA entity; a 2-fer counts as 2 entities.
roveCounting.eveningStrategy: Evening activations can intentionally count toward the current UTC rove day and reduce the next local day's required load.

vehicle.chargingNetworkRule: Prefer Tesla Superchargers only for planned charging stops; do not treat generic EV chargers as equivalent when judging feasibility.
vehicle.notes: Account for roughly 30 minutes at Tesla Superchargers after each 2.5 hours of driving. Friday likely needs charging in the CT / lower-Hudson corridor before work, then can top up near Edison / Woodbridge after the Tallman-to-NJ move.

weekendPlanning.notes: Saturday and Sunday have no work window, so they can carry more driving and more activations than weekdays.

candidateHighRoveDay.status: current working plan: make Sunday the DC Cheetah baseline day, with Ostrich as the stretch if the n-fer flow supports it

constraints.jurisdictionActivationGoal: Activate every state and Washington, DC, actually crossed on the selected route. Pennsylvania is now included because the Saturday route deliberately uses the John Heinz branch.
constraints.multiParkActivationPriority: Nice to have; do not optimize the route around 2/3/4-fers unless they are convenient.

dcActivation.timing: Handle DC on Sunday June 21 from the Foggy Bottom hotel, with an optional friend meetup if timing works.
dcActivation.notes: The friend activation is nice to have, not a route constraint. Saturday night is already in DC, so Sunday should use the local density and then continue south to the Fredericksburg hotel.

outreach.purpose: Contact common activators near short-listed target parks for practical access, parking, antenna, and operating vibe checks.
outreach.emailDraft: Hi <name>, I am planning a June 2026 POTA road trip from Rhode Island to Florida and noticed you have activated <park/reference>. I am considering it for a brief activation while passing through and would appreciate any practical advice: easy parking, good operating spots, antenna constraints, safety/access issues, and whether it is realistic for about an hour on site. Thanks and 73, Rob / N1RWJ

timelineDrafts.name: Fixed-anchor southbound plan
timelineDrafts.recommendation: Use Friday to bank RI, CT, and NY before the booked Edison overnight. Saturday starts at Cheesequake, deliberately adds John Heinz for PA, then goes to Fox Point and Fort McHenry for a post-20:00 EDT Maryland activation before the Foggy Bottom hotel.
assumptions: RI is activated early Friday morning near Hope.
CT is activated Friday morning at Silver Sands.
The work window remains 13:00-17:00, so the car should be positioned near Stamford / White Plains / Tallman before work starts.
Tallman is activated after work, then the evening drive continues to the booked Edison, NJ hotel.
Saturday covers NJ, PA, DE, and MD before the Foggy Bottom hotel; Fort McHenry is targeted for 19:30 arrival and 20:00 EDT activation start so it counts on the Sunday UTC rove day.
Sunday remains the large DC / NoVA rove day, ending at the booked Fredericksburg hotel.
Monday night is fixed in Florence, Tuesday night in or near Pooler, Wednesday night in Ocala, and Thursday night remains Ocala/family positioning.

days.focus: Activate RI and CT in the morning, work near the lower-Hudson corridor, activate Tallman after work, then sleep at the booked Edison hotel.
days.notes: Edison is close enough to Cheesequake for a simple Saturday start and has strong Tesla Supercharger coverage nearby.

days.area: Edison, NJ to Foggy Bottom, DC
days.focus: Activate Cheesequake, John Heinz, Fox Point, and Fort McHenry, then continue to the fixed Foggy Bottom hotel.
days.notes: Fort McHenry should be reached by about 19:30 for a 20:00 EDT activation start, banking MD for Sunday UTC.

days.focus: Large DC rove day for Cheetah/Ostrich planning, then continue south to the booked Fredericksburg hotel.
days.notes: Exact DC flow intentionally deferred to the next planning pass.

days.focus: Workday southbound push; NC and SC parks still need final selection.
days.notes: Monday night is booked in Florence.

days.area: Florence, SC to Pooler / Savannah, GA
days.focus: Activate Savannah National Wildlife Refuge and overnight in or near Pooler.
days.notes: Savannah NWR is listed for both SC and GA in local POTA data; exact operating-position credit still needs verification.

days.focus: Drive to Ocala and stay there Wednesday night.
days.notes: No required activation selected for this leg yet; it is a positioning day.

days.focus: Activate either Silver Springs State Park or Ocala National Forest in the morning.
days.notes: Silver Springs is much closer to Ocala; Ocala National Forest is the longer alternate.

days.area: Ocala to Singer Island / Riviera Beach
days.focus: Evening activation at John D. MacArthur Beach State Park, then Field Day weekend arrival.
days.notes: John D. MacArthur is very close to Singer Island.

areaBudgets.notes: Current Friday model uses the morning for RI and CT, then positions near Stamford / White Plains / Tallman before the 13:00 work block.
High-density corridor where multi-entity stops and evening UTC-day strategy could help a 10-entity rove goal.
Likely weekend work. Needs traffic-aware park choices and charging paired with food or a nearby activation.
Second weekend positioning block. If Sunday DC works, handle the friend activation here and keep the rest of the week southbound.
Best handled as a buffer/workday block around the DC evening activation rather than a high-mileage day.
A tight weekday segment because it must fit around the 13:00-17:00 work window unless the evening runs late.
Likely the hardest southern block. SC and GA should use very low-detour parks or multi-entity opportunities if available.
Useful arrival-buffer segment if Thursday gets long; can also carry the first Florida activation.
Keep the final Florida activation close to the route or destination unless earlier days create real slack.

planningConclusions: Friday now banks RI, CT, and NY before the Edison overnight.
Saturday starts near Cheesequake, deliberately routes through John Heinz for PA, then uses Fox Point for DE and Fort McHenry for MD.
Fort McHenry is targeted for a 19:30 arrival and 20:00 EDT activation start so it counts toward the Sunday UTC rove day.
Sunday remains the large DC / NoVA rove day; exact Cheetah/Ostrich flow is deferred to the next pass.
Monday night is fixed in Florence, Tuesday night in or near Pooler, Wednesday night in Ocala, and Florida has local Thursday/Friday targets selected.
NC and SC park choices are still open and should be selected after the DC flow is nailed down.

firstWeekendPlan.utcNote: In June EDT, the UTC day changes at 20:00 local. Friday evening activations before 20:00 count on Friday UTC; activations after 20:00 count on Saturday UTC.

fridayNightAnchors.name: Edison, NJ hotel
fridayNightAnchors.role: booked Friday overnight after the post-work Tallman activation
pros: Close enough to Cheesequake for a simple Saturday start
Strong Tesla Supercharger coverage in Edison / Woodbridge
cons: Requires a short Saturday morning hop to Cheesequake
Requires the full Tallman-to-NJ move after work
fridayNightAnchors.saturdayImpact: Edison -> Cheesequake is about 21 minutes Saturday morning.

saturdayRove.strategy: Saturday June 20 is now a fixed positioning route from the booked Edison hotel: Cheesequake for NJ, John Heinz for PA, Fox Point for DE, and Fort McHenry for MD at the UTC rollover before continuing to the Foggy Bottom hotel.

timingAssumptions.source: Google Maps MCP route checks for Edison -> Cheesequake -> John Heinz -> Fox Point -> Fort McHenry -> Foggy Bottom; park hours and exact n-fer positions still need confirmation.
timingAssumptions.fridayActivationTiming: RI and CT are now planned for Friday morning. Position near Stamford / White Plains / Tallman before the 13:00 work window, activate Tallman after work, then continue to the booked Edison overnight.
timingAssumptions.saturdayEveningTiming: Target Fort McHenry arrival around 19:30 EDT and start activating at 20:00 EDT so MD counts on Sunday UTC.
timingAssumptions.chargingStopsIncluded: Friday can use CT / lower-Hudson charging before work and Edison / Woodbridge charging near the overnight. Saturday likely needs one or two additional 30-minute Supercharger stops depending on starting charge.
timingAssumptions.accessNotes: Tallman is dawn-to-dusk, so the Friday after-work activation should start promptly and stay before dark. John Heinz NWR trails are sunrise-to-sundown and need to be a daytime stop.

timingModels.name: Fixed Saturday: Edison to Foggy Bottom via NJ/PA/DE/MD
timingModels.comfort: reasonable but timing-sensitive because Fort McHenry should not start before 20:00 EDT
entities: US-0512 John Heinz National Wildlife Refuge
US-9878 Northern Delaware Greenway National Recreation Trail if the Fox Point position verifies
US-4581 / US-4567 / US-4582 trail overlaps to verify at Fort McHenry
timingModels.notes: This is the selected Saturday route. Fort McHenry should be held until the 20:00 EDT UTC rollover.

fridayEveningOptions.name: Friday fixed: Tallman -> Edison hotel
fridayEveningOptions.route: Tallman Mountain State Park -> booked Edison, NJ hotel
fridayEveningOptions.estimatedTotalLabel: about 2h 35m after work before hotel settling/charging variation
fridayEveningOptions.comfort: good if Tallman starts promptly after work
fridayEveningOptions.notes: Tallman banks NY on Friday; Edison positions Saturday about 21 minutes from Cheesequake.

saturdayRove.restOfTripComfortRule: Saturday should protect the Foggy Bottom hotel arrival and Sunday DC rove. John Heinz is now part of the selected route; avoid adding extra nonessential stops.

saturdayOvernight.reason: Saturday is now primarily a positioning day to the fixed DC-area hotel, not a maximize-count rove day.

openQuestions: Confirm exact Fox Point operating position for the Northern Delaware Greenway overlap.
Confirm exact Fort McHenry operating position and whether it reliably counts US-0928 plus the relevant trail references.
Identify Saturday Tesla Supercharger pairings between Edison, Philadelphia / Delaware, and Baltimore / DC.
Do not start Fort McHenry before 20:00 EDT if the goal is Sunday UTC credit.

sundayDcRove.strategy: Sunday June 21 uses the Saturday 20:00 EDT Fort McHenry activation as the first Sunday-UTC stop, then follows the CWops/W8WZ DC Cheetah pattern: LBJ 7-fer, FDR, MLK, Korean, Constitution Gardens, Vietnam, WWII, Washington Monument, with Lincoln and Mason Neck as the clean Ostrich stretch.

timingAssumptions.source: CWops Solid Copy No. 193 W8WZ article, POTA API reference metadata, imported top-activator n-fer evidence, OSM/Nominatim GeoJSON attempts, and Google Maps route checks on 2026-06-10.

recommendedRoute.role: Bank Maryland and likely four total entities on the Sunday UTC day before driving to Foggy Bottom.
Primary Sunday morning jackpot; exact tree/overlap point still needs field verification.
Article-backed Mall stop; adds MLK after National Mall is already counted.
Cheetah threshold if Fort and LBJ verify.
Article-backed buffer beyond Cheetah and natural end of Mall walk.

recommendedRoute.stop: Mason Neck State Park / Mason Neck NWR
recommendedRoute.role: Southbound 2-fer that can push the day to Ostrich if reached before dusk.

timingModels.name: Cheetah baseline: Fort + LBJ + Mall through Vietnam
timingModels.estimatedTotalLabel: about 8h 45m split across Saturday evening and Sunday, before food/parking variance
timingModels.comfort: reasonable if exact n-fer positions verify
timingModels.notes: Cheetah threshold is reached at Vietnam if Fort McHenry 4-fer and LBJ 7-fer verify.

timingModels.name: Full W8WZ route plus Fort McHenry
timingModels.estimatedTotalLabel: about 10h 45m split across Saturday evening and Sunday
timingModels.comfort: reasonable if exact n-fer positions verify
timingModels.notes: This mirrors the article sequence and leaves room for one southbound add-on if the day is moving well.

timingModels.name: Ostrich stretch: full route + Lincoln + Mason Neck
timingModels.estimatedTotalLabel: about 14h 10m split across Saturday evening and Sunday; stretch but plausible with disciplined 45-60 minute stops
timingModels.notes: Mason Neck should be before dusk; this model may need a morning start and no long social pause.

timingModels.name: Ostrich high-confidence buffer: add Dyke Marsh before Mason Neck
timingModels.estimatedTotalLabel: about 15h 40m split across Saturday evening and Sunday; likely too heavy unless using shorter activations
timingModels.notes: Dyke Marsh adds margin but also pushes Mason Neck later; keep as backup unless exact access and pace are excellent.

openQuestions: Confirm the exact LBJ 7-fer tree/overlap point against imagery or with a local activator before relying on all seven references.
Confirm Fort McHenry exact position for US-4582 in addition to US-0928, US-4581, and US-4567.
Confirm Mason Neck NWR operating restrictions: POTA cache says free-standing antennas only, no vegetation attachment, no off-trail operating, and no tables in parking areas.
Check closing/dusk times for Mason Neck if using it as the Ostrich add-on.
Decide whether the friend meetup happens inside the rove sequence or after the Cheetah count is already secure.

mdTarget.meaning: Arrive near Fort McHenry around 19:30 and start the activation at or after 20:00 EDT so Maryland counts toward the Sunday UTC rove day.
defaultRouteBeforeTarget: US-6992 or nearby RI park Friday morning
Booked Edison, NJ hotel Friday night
US-0928 Fort McHenry Saturday evening at/after 20:00 EDT
mdTarget.optionalBranch: None for PA now: John Heinz is part of the selected Saturday route.

nferEvidence.sourceDescription: Candidate n-fer locations inferred from submissions by top activators.
nferEvidence.currentRead: Useful for finding proven multi-reference positions, but treat it as evidence to verify rather than proof that an activation will work from any exact parking spot.

saturday.summary: The data does not validate the assumed Gateway/Fort Wadsworth 2-fer.
saturday.notes: The current Saturday stretch should not rely on this pair until activator outreach or another source confirms it.

saturday.summary: Gateway National Recreation Area plus New Jersey Coastal State Trail appears as a data-backed 2-fer.
saturday.routingNote: Google Maps routing from Palisades to Sandy Hook Gateway to Cheesequake is about 2h total drive time, so this may be too expensive for the Saturday UTC target unless it replaces the Staten Island idea and the day is running early.

sunday.summary: The February 2026 CWops Solid Copy article describes an exact LBJ Memorial Grove position in DC that counts as a 7-fer.
sunday.routingNote: Foggy Bottom to LBJ Grove should be a short local hop; the nearby parking-lot/picnic-table option is described as a 4-fer if the exact 7-fer walk-out spot is not practical.

sunday.summary: Fox Point State Park plus Northern Delaware Greenway National Recreation Trail is a Delaware 2-fer candidate near I-95.
Fort McHenry has repeated n-fer evidence with Star-Spangled Banner and Captain John Smith Chesapeake trails.
sunday.routingNote: Central NJ to Fox Point to Fort McHenry to Rock Creek to Mason Neck is about 4h31m of driving before activation time.

sunday.summary: Mason Neck State Park plus Elizabeth Hartwell Mason Neck NWR is a Virginia 2-fer candidate for the Sunday southbound line.
sunday.routingNote: Rock Creek Park to Mason Neck State Park is roughly 55-63 minutes depending on the exact route.
